topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box was heavy, the petals a vibrant, almost aggressive shade of crimson. I stood by the front door, my hands trembling slightly as I lifted the wrapping paper. Inside, a lavish arrangement of roses, perfectly formed and undeniably expensive. There was no note, just the scent of rich perfume filling the hallway. My gaze darted around the empty living room, cataloging every misplaced book, every slightly crooked picture frame, as if searching for some flaw that justified this unexpected gesture. I tucked the flowers awkwardly under my arm, the weight pressing against my ribs, and tried to make myself small, hoping the sudden attention—even this silent, floral one—would simply vanish.

[story 2]

When the delivery person handed me the bouquet, the smile they offered felt too bright, too knowing. I took the arrangement with a stiff formality, my fingers brushing against the cool stems. I clutched the paper bag tight, my knuckles turning white. Every eye in the apartment seemed to be fixed on the flowers, and I felt a sudden, intense heat rising up my neck. *Why?* The thought echoed hollowly in my mind. I retreated immediately to the kitchen, setting the flowers down on the counter as if they might contaminate the space, needing a moment to regulate the frantic fluttering beneath my sternum before I could even consider where to put them.

[story 3]

The flowers arrived on a Tuesday afternoon, a stark contrast to the usual mundane rhythm of my week. I found them sitting on the entryway table, a silent declaration of some unseen affection. I approached them slowly, my steps feeling clumsy on the hardwood floor. I leaned in, inhaling deeply, trying to anchor myself in the sheer beauty of the arrangement, but the attention felt like a spotlight. I kept my eyes fixed on the arrangement, avoiding any direct contact with the person who must have sent them, my posture tightening until I felt rigid. I mumbled a weak, "Thank you," the words sounding thin and inadequate against the backdrop of the opulent blooms.
